title: bridelia scleroneura
intro: Bridelia scleroneura, also known as the "False Coralloid," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Bridelia scleroneura include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and respiratory problems. Bridelia scleroneura can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine as an ointment or poultice to treat wounds and skin infections.
